beats.activity: Wakes up, makes a slow cup of coffee, and checks the dog’s water and breakfast before starting the day indoors.; Does a quick tidy and sets out a filming lens pouch and sourdough starter jar for later.
beats.reasoning: Rain starts early, so she keeps her morning quiet at home to lower anxiety.

beats.activity: Drives toward her shift, taking a cautious route to avoid the SR-520 eastbound closure window.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 is closed eastbound 9am–noon, so she leaves earlier and drives defensively in wet conditions.

beats.activity: Opens and runs the bar: preheats equipment, checks beans and milk, and manages a steady line through the drizzle.
beats.location_name: Third-wave Coffee Shop (Downtown Kirkland, Kirkland, WA)
beats.reasoning: Her job is the anchor of the day, and the rain keeps customers nearby and makes the shop feel cozy.

beats.activity: Grabs a warm meal quickly and steps into a quieter corner to eat slowly and reset.
beats.reasoning: After a long shift, she chooses a low-crowd, indoor spot to match her introverted pace.

beats.activity: Picks up a couple of grocery essentials for baking (flour, yeast, and eggs) under the store awning to stay dry.
beats.reasoning: Rain discourages walking; she does a fast, practical stop close to where she’s already working.

beats.activity: Drives back across the lake with extra buffer to account for lingering traffic from earlier SR-520 disruptions.
beats.reasoning: Even after the closure window, wet-road delays and residual congestion make her plan conservative.

beats.activity: Unloads groceries, lets the dog stretch inside briefly, and washes up before starting baking.
beats.reasoning: She decompresses at home immediately, keeping social energy low after a long shift.

beats.activity: Bakes sourdough: feeds starter, mixes dough, does folds, and sets it to rise while she listens to rain.
beats.reasoning: Her evening routine centers on sourdough and the steady weather makes the process feel extra grounding.

beats.activity: Takes a short, sheltered photo walk with her camera around nearby streets and porch lighting—staying close to home.
beats.reasoning: She still wants film photography time, but rain means she keeps it brief and nearby.

beats.activity: Returns home, talks to her dog, and plates a simple dinner while the dough finishes its schedule.
beats.reasoning: Introverted downtime at home helps her wind down after work and keeps the evening calm.

diary:
Woke up to that soft, steady drumming of rain on the windows in Issaquah. I made my coffee slow, checked the dog’s water, and did a quick little tidy—enough to feel like I had control over something, even with the day already feeling grey. Before heading out, I grabbed my filming lens pouch and the sourdough starter jar, because if the weather is going to be stubborn, I might as well be too. 

The drive toward Downtown Kirkland was cautious; SR-520 being closed eastbound from 9 to noon had everyone on edge, like the whole lake route was holding its breath. At the shop, I ran the bar through the drizzle—preheating, dialing in milk, keeping the line moving while the rain kept whispering outside the glass. Later I ducked into a quieter corner at Paseo Coffee to eat and reset. I grabbed flour, yeast, and eggs at QFC under the awning, then baked sourdough back home while the dog curled up nearby. After dinner, I took a short photo walk by Front Street Park, catching porch lights through the mist. Tonight feels calm, the kind that settles in.
thought_process: I planned my morning around the SR-520 eastbound closure window, so I left early and routed around the backups, which kept me from starting work frazzled. At the shop, I leaned into the predictable rhythm—preheat on time, check beans and milk, keep drinks consistent—because the drizzle already made people a little slower and more chatty; a steady pace mattered more than flashy speed. When I stepped into Paseo Coffee for lunch, I chose a corner to give myself quiet instead of forcing conversation, letting my mood reset before the afternoon rush. I stocked baking supplies at QFC under the awning because the rain wasn’t going to let up, and sourdough felt like a long, patient counterweight. By evening, traffic echoes and the grey sky faded into something cozy rather than stressful.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
